meta.title: Podiatry Practice Software | Diabetic Foot RTM & Wound Tracking | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to podiatry practice operations. $7,800/month in diabetic foot RTM. Wound healing tracking. DME coordination. Surgical authorization.

hero.subhead: Your diabetic foot patients need monitoring between visits — that's billable RTM. Your wounds need healing progression tracking. Your DME needs coordination. clinIQ captures podiatric RTM, tracks wound healing, and coordinates diabetic foot care.

sections.content:
Podiatry practices manage chronic conditions (diabetic foot, wounds) while performing high-volume procedures.

**The diabetic foot opportunity:**

Diabetic patients should monitor: daily foot inspections, new wounds or changes, pain or numbness, temperature differences. This monitoring prevents amputations.

60 diabetic foot patients × $130/month = $7,800/month in RTM.

**The wound tracking problem:**

Wounds heal over weeks to months. Progress must be documented: size, depth, drainage, tissue type. Without systematic tracking, healing stalls go unnoticed.

**The DME coordination:**

Diabetic shoes, custom orthotics, offloading devices — DME requires fitting, ordering, and delivery coordination.

Diabetic foot patients benefit from between-visit monitoring.

**What patients track:**

- Daily foot inspection (any new wounds, redness, swelling)
- Pain or numbness changes
- Temperature differences between feet
- Compliance with offloading
- Blood glucose (for correlation)

**What clinIQ does:**

Diabetic foot templates: Appropriate monitoring questions.

Alert thresholds: New wound? Temperature asymmetry? Immediate alerts.

Photo submission: Patients can submit foot photos for review.

Compliance tracking: Offloading device use, shoe wear, activity modification.

Wound documentation is critical for outcomes and billing.

**What clinIQ tracks:**

Wound measurements: Length, width, depth at each visit.

Healing progression: Size reduction over time graphed.

Tissue characteristics: Granulation, slough, necrosis percentages.

Treatment applied: Debridement, dressings, advanced therapies.

**The clinical value:**

Wounds that aren't improving need treatment escalation. Systematic tracking identifies stalled wounds early.

Podiatric DME includes:

- Diabetic shoes and inserts (Medicare DMEPOS)
- Custom orthotics
- Offloading devices (CAM boots, total contact casts)
- Compression stockings

**What clinIQ does:**

DME pipeline: Order → fitting → authorization → delivery → follow-up.

Diabetic shoe program: Eligibility verification, fitting, ordering.

Orthotic tracking: Mold/scan → fabrication → fitting → adjustment.

Podiatric surgery (bunionectomy, hammertoe, reconstructive) often requires prior authorization.

**What clinIQ does:**

Auth tracking: Status per patient per procedure.

Conservative care documentation: Prior treatment documented for auth support.

Pre-op coordination: Labs, clearance, imaging tracked.

**Week 1-2: Configuration and Training**

Diabetic RTM, wound tracking, DME coordination configured.

**ROI Math:**

RTM revenue: 60 patients × $130/month = $7,800/month = $93,600/year

Wound tracking: Better outcomes, justified billing.

DME coordination: Improved compliance and revenue capture.

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

faqs.a: Yes. Diabetic foot monitoring qualifies for RTM codes 98977 and 98980.

faqs.q: How do you track wound measurements?
faqs.a: Length, width, depth entered at each visit. Healing progression graphed over time.

faqs.q: Does this work with diabetic shoe programs?
faqs.a: Yes. Eligibility, fitting, ordering, and delivery tracked.
